commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because this is a protected maintainer draft touching active TUI state-machine paths with related overlapping PRs; no narrow automated repair remains after the latest abort cleanup.; Cleared: The diff is limited to TUI state/rendering code and tests; it does not change dependencies, workflows, secrets, package scripts, permissions, or external code execution paths. (sha=70d2a28e98da0568870b344b325a7614c5a3cf5e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes TUI chat-log and state handlers so submitted prompts become pending user rows that survive history rebuilds, rekey across accepted run IDs, reconcile with persisted history, and clean up on failed or early-aborted sends, with focused TUI coverage.

PR surface: Source +81, Tests +207. Total +288 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main still renders outbound text as a normal chat-log user row and later clears the whole chat log during history rebuild. I did not run a live TUI repro in this read-only review, but the source path and related reports are high-confidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because this is a protected maintainer draft touching active TUI state-machine paths with related overlapping PRs; no narrow automated repair remains after the latest abort cleanup.

target.title: Preserve TUI optimistic user messages across history reloads
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve optimistic outbound TUI user messages as pending chat-log entries instead of plain rendered entries.
- Keep pending user messages across `chat.history` rebuilds, reconcile them once persisted history catches up, and clear only the failed pending send on `chat.send` errors.
- Add focused TUI regression coverage plus an Unreleased changelog entry.

## Related Issues

Refs #54722 and #59014 for the earlier closed TUI user-message disappearance regressions. Related but distinct from #78017, which tracks long-message scrollback loss in the underlying renderer.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: TUI user messages no longer disappear when a stale history reload or run-error refresh happens before the optimistic outbound message is present in persisted history.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, TUI unit config and fake-backend PTY harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui.config.ts src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Evidence after fix: focused TUI tests passed 4 files / 115 tests; fake-backend PTY lane passed 1 file / 11 tests; `git diff --check` passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: optimistic user messages are kept through stale history rebuilds and committed/reconciled once the real run or persisted history catches up.

What was not tested: no live Gateway/provider run was executed; the fake-backend PTY harness does not prove Gateway transport, embedded runtime, providers, session persistence, or live streaming.
target.author: romneyda

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: RomneyDa
commands.review_summary:
- Removed the `CHANGELOG.md` edit from this contributor PR.
- Kept optimistic user messages pending after the first gateway run binding; they are now removed only when `chat.history` reconciliation sees the persisted user message or when `chat.send` fails explicitly.
- Added regressions for stale history rebuilds, run-binding retention, and send-failure cleanup.
